own_review_points: Real test bug: delayBeforeTrailersHttp2() calls trailers(HTTP_2) instead of delayBeforeTrailers(HTTP_2) — the HTTP/2 trailers-delay path is never exercised
New BufferedSource.skipAll() overload has no timeout (unlike Source.skipAll(duration,unit)) — Response.trailers() can block indefinitely on a large unread body
TRAILERS_RESPONSE_BODY_TRUNCATED sentinel uses a fake header (OkHttp-Response-Body: Truncated) as an internal marker — design smell
trailers() now throws IOException (was IllegalStateException 'too early') on truncated body — behavioral change
Http2ExchangeCodec.isResponseComplete returns false when stream is null; semantics worth a comment
Human nit: readTrailersAfterEarlyEofAndClose test name is over-differentiated / misleading
notes: Excellent lead catch: the delayBeforeTrailersHttp2 copy-paste bug (calls trailers() not delayBeforeTrailers()) is a genuine, verifiable test defect that silently un-tests the HTTP/2 delay path — critical. skipAll-no-timeout, the sentinel-header design smell, the IllegalStateException->IOException behavioral/doc change, and the nullable-stream isResponseComplete note are all grounded good points. body-discard restatement and the @Disabled-tests comment ask are trivial. Missed only the human's lone test-name nit. Much deeper than the human reference.
